literal: non-duality of the indivisible; a-vibhāga (non-division, indivisibility) + advaita (non-duality)
invariant_definition: Avibhāgādvaita ("non-duality of indivision") is the Vedānta position associated with Vijñānabhikṣu (16th c.), who sought to harmonize Vedānta with Sāṅkhya and Yoga. On this view Brahman, souls, and world are neither absolutely identical (as in Śaṅkara's Advaita) nor absolutely different, but stand in a relation of inseparable non-division (avibhāga): the plurality is real, yet the parts are never severable from the whole. The position is a form of bhedābheda that stresses the undivided continuity of the manifold with Brahman, reading the apparent distinctness of selves and matter as differentiation within an indivisible reality rather than as separate substances or as mere appearance.
